rows.key_dev: Fitch placed AT&T BBB+ on Rating Watch Negative in 2025; AT&T completed EchoStar acquisition and guides $18B+ FCF for 2026 with target net debt/EBITDA ~2.5x.
Verizon Q1 2026 beat: EPS $1.28 vs est, record adj. EBITDA $13.4B; first positive postpaid phone net adds since 2013. Frontier integration on track; May 2026 hybrid note issuance at Baa2/BBB-/BBB.
No material news.
Disney renewed $9.25B credit facilities (364-day $5.25B + 5-year $4B) in March 2026; streaming momentum continues with FY2026 parks and DTC execution on track.

Paramount's $110B acquisition of WBD pending regulatory approval (est. Q3 2026 close); combined entity net debt ~$79B. S&P will lower Paramount to BB upon close; WBD consent solicitation on note amendments filed May 2026.
Netflix withdrew from WBD studio/streaming acquisition (Feb 2026) after Paramount outbid; Q1 2026 results strong; company guides 150M streaming subs by end-2026.
Amazon reported Q1 2026 earnings Apr 29; AWS growth accelerating on AI demand. Capex trajectory remains elevated for AI infrastructure build-out.
Alphabet Q1 2026 earnings Apr 29 beat; Google Cloud +48% YoY; $175-185B capex guide for 2026, more than double 2025. AI monetization remains key investor focus.
Microsoft Q3 FY26 earnings reported Apr 29; Azure CC growth 37-38%; AI Copilot monetization accelerating. FY2026 capex elevated for AI data center build-out.
Moody's and S&P both maintain Negative outlook; adj. debt/EBITDA >4x, negative FCF as Oracle invests heavily in AI cloud infrastructure. RPO backlog hit $523B in Dec 2025.
Salesforce FQ1 2027 earnings due Jun 4, 2026; Agentforce AI platform driving pipeline growth. No material adverse credit news in past 60 days.
IBM acquiring Confluent (announced Dec 2025) for ~$11B using cash; expected to close mid-2026. Moody's noted acquisition at ~10x Confluent's 2025 revenue; moderately leveraging.
Apple fiscal Q2 2026 results reported Apr 30; guided Q2 revenue growth 13-16%. Services revenue growth and tariff supply chain impacts remain key investor focus.
No material news.
S&P and Fitch maintain Positive outlook on Broadcom driven by VMware integration synergies and strong AI networking/custom ASIC revenue growth trajectory.
No material news.
HPE selected by TD Synnex as global distribution partner for its unified model. No material adverse credit news in past 60 days.
No material news.
Nextracker (now Nextpower) achieved investment-grade credit rating in Q3 FY26; rebranded, Q4 FY26 earnings reported ~May 2026; FY2026 revenue +20% YoY, record backlog >$5.25B.
No material news.
Announced spin-off of data center EMS business (~$6.6Bn revenue) and acquisition of Electrical Power Products. S&P affirmed BBB-/Stable May 6 2026; pro forma leverage expected to remain below 3x.
No material news.
Arrow Q1 2026 revenue $9.5B, up 39% YoY, beat high end of guidance; EPS $4.55 GAAP, $5.22 non-GAAP; strong EMEA ECS growth +46% YoY.
No material news.
TD Synnex Q1 FY26 record results: revenue $17.2B (+18% YoY), non-GAAP EPS $4.73 (+69% YoY); Hyve AI segment gross billings +95% YoY driven by hyperscaler demand.
Ingram Micro Q4 2025 net sales $14.88B, up 11.5% YoY; adj. FCF $1.63B for quarter; highest quarterly FCF in over a decade. Listed on NYSE (INGM) in 2024.
CDW Q1 2026 results reported; no material adverse credit news. AI-driven IT refresh cycle supporting demand outlook.
Kyndryl FY2026 results (May 6): revenue $15.1B flat YoY, adj. EBITDA $2.7B (+6%); FCF $406M; investment grade from all three agencies. FY2027 guidance provided.
No material news.
S&P rated Equinix unsecured notes BBB+ (Feb 2026); 60% of Q4 2025 largest deals AI-driven; record 2025 capacity of 23,250 racks. Strong 2026 AI demand pipeline.
Digital Realty Q4 2025 revenue $1.6B (+14% YoY); FY2025 revenue $6.1B, adj. EBITDA $3.34B; guides 2026 revenue $6.6-6.7B; record leasing and strong AI-driven backlog.
American Tower guides 3.5% organic growth in 2026; owns 148,000+ communications sites; raised quarterly dividend 4.9% to $1.70/share. AI inference edge demand supports outlook.
PayPal appointed Enrique Lores as new CEO (effective Mar 1, 2026); issued $2B notes May 12, 2026 (A3/A-); Q1 2026 solid results but new leadership conducting strategic review.
Corpay Q1 2026: revenue $1.26B (+25% YoY), adj. EPS $5.80 (+29%); Alpha Group and AvidXchange minority stake acquired; PayByPhone divested. Q2 2026 guidance $1.295B revenue (+18%).
No material news.
No material news.

Lagardère (Vivendi subsidiary, majority owned) faces French Supreme Court hearing May 22, 2026 on Bolloré governance dispute; Vivendi's loan to Lagardère stands at €450M; debt refinanced with 5-yr €700M RCF.
Q1 2026 EPS of $1.14 beat estimates; gross bookings +15% to $53.8B. Next earnings confirmed Aug 5, 2026.
Q1 2026 gross bookings +25% YoY to $53.7B; non-GAAP EPS up 44% YoY. CFO change effective Feb 2026.
Q1 2026 EPS $0.64 beat, revenue record $14.2B +9.4% YoY; guiding to ~$1B pre-tax profit Q2 despite fuel spike.
Record Q1 2026 results; launched $2.5B buyback; announced PROPEL targets through 2029. One notch from IG at S&P/Fitch.
Q1 2026 adj EPS $3.60 beat; returned $1.1B to shareholders via buybacks/dividends; FY adj EPS guided $17.10–$17.50.
Net leverage 5.3x as of Q1 2026; below optimal booking range due to geopolitical/Middle East headwinds impacting Europe summer demand.
Exposed to Middle East conflict escalation and Iran war affecting regional routes; geopolitical risk elevated for Turkish carrier.
Q1 2026 EPS $3.70 beat; raised FY guidance to $13.5–$15.5B EBIT adj on $500M tariff Supreme Court refund; $1.1B EV write-down.
Q1 2026 global deliveries +6.3% YoY; auto margins ex-credits improved to 19.2%; guiding >$25B CapEx for 2025–2026 on factories/AI.
S&P maintains Negative outlook; Q1 2026 revenue $43.3B; restructuring underway and Novelis supply disruption cost ~$1B in 2025.
FY2026 results announced May 8; $9.1B tariff headwind projected; Q1 2026 US sales -0.1% YoY. FY earnings impacted by US tariffs.
Deep restructuring underway; US Q1 2026 sales -7.5% YoY; multiple negative outlook from all three agencies; merger talks with Honda failed.
Tariff headwinds from US auto tariffs; EU-US trade negotiations ongoing. BMW remains highly rated with strong capital buffer.
Moody's and S&P Negative outlooks on weaker profitability; US tariffs adding billions in cost; China market share declining vs. BYD/Geely.
Q1 2026 impacted by US tariffs; China premium market competitive pressure persists. Strong balance sheet with net cash position.
US Q1 2026 sales record; all three agencies upgraded to 'A' category in 2024. Absorbing US tariffs while expanding US manufacturing capacity.
Q1 2026 revenue $22.2B +14% YoY; 143 deliveries (first quarterly win vs Airbus since 2019); $47.2B debt; still loss-making in BCA.
Q1 2026 orders +87%, revenue +29%; adj EPS $1.86 +25% YoY; FCF $1.7B +14%; trending to high end of FY 2026 guidance.
Global truck market softness continues; tariff exposure on North American and EU trucks. Q1 2026 volumes down YoY on market cycle.
Resilient parts business and financial services support earnings; investment-grade rated; trucking market softness a near-term headwind.
Atmus spin-off completed; Accelera electrification strategy on track. Q1 2026 results solid despite softer global truck demand.
Strong backlog in freight and transit segments; Q1 2026 results in line with expectations. No material credit events in 60 days.
Negative outlooks from all three agencies; ag/construction equipment demand weakness persisting; restructuring and cost-cutting underway.
No material news in past 60 days. Q1 FY2027 results due mid-Aug 2026; strong market share gains amid tariff-driven trade-down.
Q2 FY2026 SSS +3.3%; EPS $27.63; $310.8M buyback in quarter. Domestic auto parts demand robust amid higher used vehicle prices.
No material news. Auto parts and industrial distribution segments performing steadily; benefiting from aging vehicle fleet trend.
No material news. Membership renewal rates remain strong; tariff uncertainty driving trade-down benefit; Q3 FY2026 results due Jun 5.
Q1 FY2026 sales $41.8B +4.8% YoY; comp sales +0.6%; adj EPS $3.43 in line; reaffirmed FY2026 guidance with 2.5–4.5% sales growth.
No material news. Strong DIFM demand; aging vehicle fleet and high new car prices driving aftermarket tailwinds. Q2 results due Jul 23.
No material news in past 60 days. Travel retail recovery ongoing; North American airport expansion strategy progressing.
Strategic review of non-core assets ongoing; MBO/privatization proposal from founding family; Alimentation Couche-Tard takeover bid withdrawn.
Q1 2026 EPS $0.91 +18% YoY; comparable EPS $0.86 +18%; FCF $1.8B; gained NARTD market share globally. Next earnings Jul 23.
No material news in past 60 days. Largest US Coke bottler; benefiting from pricing power and volume stability in core geographies.
Chairman change at Apr 2026 AGM; retiring CEO William Gifford (Altria) proposed as new director. Q1 2026 EPS beat +8.34% vs estimates.
Issued new bonds May 2026; IQOS smoke-free volumes growing strongly; FY2026 guidance raised on strong Swedish Match/ZYN performance.
Q1 2026 adj EPS $1.32 beat by 5.6%; revenue $4.76B vs $4.58B est; on! nicotine pouch shipments +25%; stock +6.77% on results May 13.
No material news in past 60 days. Next-gen products (blu, Zone X) growing; consistent dividend; FY results expected Nov 2026.
No material news in past 60 days. Leaf tobacco volumes stable; ingredients/specialty business diversification progressing steadily.
Moody's downgraded Nike to A2 (Nov 2025) and S&P to A+ (Jul 2025) citing profit deterioration; Q3 FY2026 beat estimates but revenue remains pressured by tariffs and turnaround costs.
Q1 2026 EPS of $1.97 beat estimates; company reaffirmed organic sales growth for 2026 and targets margin recovery in H2 with ~6% productivity gains.
Triple sub-IG rating with all three agencies on Negative outlook; Q1 2026 EPS of -$0.56 badly missed, FCF -$896M; common dividend suspended to prioritize deleveraging. $1.08B equity raise completed.
Q1 2026 EPS of $0.44 vs $0.31 prior year; restructuring actions initiated in Q4 2025 ongoing; CFO Zaramella elevated to COO role in Jan 2026 management change.
Q1 2026 comparable EPS of $0.94 beat $0.86 consensus; revenue up ~16% YoY to $3.6B on strong beverage can demand; no explicit forward guidance issued.
Q1 2026 adjusted EPS +11% to $1.86; global beverage shipments up 5%; dividend raised 35% and new India greenfield plant announced; 2026 FCF guidance $900M reiterated.
No material news.
Q1 2026 EPS of $0.97 beat $0.94 estimate; revenue $5.32B above forecast; full-year EPS guidance maintained but gross margin outlook lowered due to raw material inflation and tariffs.
Q1 2026 adjusted diluted EPS $0.32 vs $0.24 prior year; Self Care organic sales declined 2.3% due to weak cold/flu season; company tracking toward full-year targets.
No material news in the last 60 days; Coty continues executing deleveraging plan with ongoing brand portfolio optimization targeting leverage reduction toward 4x.
Q4 2025 EPS $0.87 beat $0.81 estimate; company under ongoing DOJ/SEC investigation into accounting irregularities; CFO transition and negative rating outlooks across all three agencies persist.

JBS pursuing dual-listing on NYSE (JBSS3 Brazil + proposed US listing); ongoing regulatory review and governance scrutiny related to Batista family control remain credit watch items.
Q1 2026 net sales $4.5B; adjusted EBITDA margin 6.8%; U.S. segment EBITDA 7.0%; company executing product mix upgrades in Big Bird and growing key customer partnerships.

Moody's placed Baa2 on review for downgrade in Sep 2025 following planned business split announcement; North America EVP Pedro Navio departed Feb 2026; Q1 2026 EPS $0.67 with improving market share trends.

Full-year 2025 results reported Apr 22, 2026; revenue growth driven by luxury and premium optics segments; next detailed semi-annual report due Sep 2026.
Q1 2026 beat expectations; 2026 adjusted diluted EPS guidance raised to $14.08-$14.28; Skyrizi and Rinvoq combined sales tracking toward >$31B in 2026; pipeline advancing across multiple stages.
Continued strong GLP-1 franchise growth; Q4 2025 revenue +43% YoY, fifth consecutive quarter of top industry growth; manufacturing capacity expansion ongoing.

All three agencies carry Negative outlook near sub-IG threshold; ongoing Roundup litigation liability and Crop Science underperformance weigh heavily; glofitamab/asundexian pipeline updates monitored closely.
Q2 FY2026 revenue +19% to $65.6B; non-GAAP EPS +36% to $2.63; FY2026 EPS guidance raised to $10.15-$10.35; specialty revenue on track to surpass $50B; leverage within target range.
Q2 FY2026 operating income growth in both U.S. and International segments; OneOncology integration progressing; GLP-1 category growth moderating but specialty logistics remains resilient.
Q3 FY2026 adjusted EPS $9.34 (+16% YoY); FY2026 guidance raised to $38.80-$39.20; strong specialty distribution and oncology platform driving above-market growth.
No material news.
Termite transformation program and North America pest control integration ongoing; management executing on efficiency plan post-Terminix acquisition; leverage reduction remains strategic priority.
No material news in the last 60 days; Brink's executing on operational efficiency and digital cash management expansion; leverage remains elevated but within guidance targets.
Q1 2026 revenue $1.55B; net income $311.8M; $1.67B in property investments; net debt/EBITDAre 5.2x (improved from 5.3x); 15,571 properties at 98.9% occupancy; monthly dividend maintained.
Chevron reported Q1 2026 adjusted earnings of $2.8B; production up 15% YoY driven by Hess acquisition and Permian growth. Raised quarterly dividend 4% to $1.78/share.

BP agreed to sell Gelsenkirchen refinery to Klesch Group (Mar 2026), raising structural cost-reduction target to $6.5-7.5B by 2027. Ongoing portfolio simplification amid negative outlooks from all three agencies.

ExxonMobil reported Q1 2026 earnings of $4.2B ($8.8B ex-timing effects); declared Q2 dividend of $1.03/share. Golden Pass Train 1 achieved first LNG; Permian production on track for 1.8 MMboe/d in 2026.

Q1 2026 adjusted earnings of $200M vs $1.0B in Q4 2025, impacted by $839M mark-to-market hedge losses. Increased annualized quarterly dividend by 7%; raised Sweeny fractionation and Freeport LPG capacity.
Q1 2026 revenue up 7% QoQ to $3.26B; Scarborough Energy Project 96% complete targeting first LNG in Q4 2026. New CEO conducting organisational efficiency review. Liquidity at $8.3B.
Q1 2026 adjusted net income $3.7B, up >100% YoY; total equity production 2,313 mboe/d up 9% YoY. Strong trading performance amid Middle East/Hormuz volatility.
Shell agreed to acquire ARC Resources for ~$16.4B EV (Apr 2026), adding 370 kboe/d and targeting 4% production CAGR through 2030. Q1 2026 adjusted earnings $6.9B; $3.0B buyback launched May 2026.
Q1 2026 net income $5.4B, up 29% YoY driven by exceptional crude trading performance during Hormuz disruption. Signed EGAS offshore exploration cooperation agreement in Egypt (May 2026).

Halliburton FY2025 revenue fell 3.2% and operating income dropped ~40% amid softer North America activity. YPF awarded Halliburton a multibillion-dollar long-term unconventional completions contract in Argentina (Apr 2026).

SLB Q1 2026 revenue $8.72B, up 3% YoY; EPS ex-charges $0.52, down 28% YoY. Raised quarterly dividend 3.5% to $0.295/share; committed to $4B+ shareholder returns for 2026. Macro headwinds in international markets.

BKR Q1 2026 IET RPO hit record $33.1B; IET EBITDA up 35% YoY. Extended long-term service agreement with Cheniere covering Trains 8 and 9 at Corpus Christi. Strong IET order book with 1.5x book-to-bill.

TechnipFMC secured subsea contract for the Hammerhead project. Zacks highlighted FTI as well-positioned for industry tailwinds in March 2026. Strong subsea order backlog supports multi-year revenue visibility.
Cheniere reported Q1 2026 revenue ~$5.9B and raised full-year 2026 financial guidance (May 7). Corpus Christi Stage 3 progressing; >95% LNG volumes contracted for next decade.

Enbridge reported strong Q1 2026 results, reaffirmed 2026 financial guidance, and grew secured project backlog to $40B (May 8). Predictable utility-like model with 98% regulated/contracted cash flows.

NextEra announced $67B all-stock merger with Dominion Energy on May 18, 2026, creating world's largest regulated electric utility. Ratings reaffirmed with stable outlook expected post-close; regulatory approval required.
Parent NEE announced transformative merger with Dominion Energy (May 18, 2026); NEP's strategic direction and distribution policy under review given combined company's regulated growth focus.
No material news.
Sempra reported Q1 2026 GAAP earnings $1.04B ($1.58/diluted share), up from $0.91B in Q1 2025. Capital recycling initiatives ongoing at Sempra Infrastructure; $65B capital plan reaffirmed.
No material news in last 60 days. AES continues execution of US renewable energy pipeline; below-investment-grade ratings reflect elevated leverage at the holding company level.

Dominion agreed to all-stock merger with NextEra Energy (May 18, 2026) in ~$67B deal; Dominion expected to benefit from improved credit ratings post-close. Dominion data center pipeline at ~51 GW in Virginia.

Edison CEO warned (May 2026) that without California wildfire liability reform in 2026, credit rating downgrades are likely for utilities. SCE faces $38-41B five-year capex plan; wildfire litigation exposure ongoing.
No material news. Constellation continues to benefit from strong nuclear power demand driven by data center/AI load growth; positive outlooks across agencies reflect improving credit profile.

No material news. RWE continues expansion of renewables portfolio in US, Europe and Australia with multi-year capex plan.

Uniper H1 2025 adjusted EBITDA fell 78% to €379M on lower generation and trading activity. Company remains state-owned post-bailout; earnings remain volatile with power generation margins under pressure.

No material news. Enel continues execution of 2024-2026 strategic plan focused on renewables expansion, deleveraging, and asset disposals.
TotalEnergies holds 50% interest in Clearway Energy Group. No standalone material credit news; company continues operating wind and solar portfolio across the US.

Brookfield Renewable reported record Q1 2026 results; acquired Boralex expanding global renewable platform. Agreed to sell ~$3B of assets in Q1 via capital recycling; advancing nuclear deployment at Westinghouse.

Fluence Q2 FY2026 revenue $465M (+8% YoY); record $5.6B backlog; signed MSAs with two hyperscalers for data center storage. Adjusted EBITDA still negative at -$9M; supply chain issues deferred $80M revenue to Q3.

Amrize (spun off from Holcim in early 2025) reported Q1 2026 earnings and continues US building materials operations. Limited history as independent public company; no material credit events in last 60 days.

top3.name: Braskem
